subject: DevOps
title: What Is a Container Registry
description: A short lesson to help you apply What Is a Container Registry.

hook.text: You built an image on your laptop. How does it get to the server in production? A registry moves it.

buildup.text: A container registry is like a library for images. Push images in, pull images out. Organized by name and tag.

discovery.text: Docker Hub is the biggest public registry. Millions of images: nginx, postgres, node — ready to pull and run.

twist.text: Companies use private registries for proprietary code. AWS ECR, Google GCR, and GitHub Packages are popular.

climax.text: The pipeline pushes an image to the registry. The deploy step pulls that exact image. No rebuilds, no drift.

punchline.text: Registries store and distribute images. Push once, pull anywhere.

quiz.question: What is the purpose of a container registry?
options: To store and distribute container images
To compile source code into binaries
To monitor running containers in production
